items.title: Report back in 90 days on worker-cooperative support, succession planning, and vendor outreach
items.summary: A policy order asking the City Manager to report back within 90 days on updates to succession-planning and employee-ownership materials, the worker-cooperative technical-assistance referral pipeline, guidance on below-threshold quote-seeking from worker-owned cooperatives, and other low-cost steps to expand support for worker-owned cooperatives. Adopted 8-0-1 (Simmons absent).
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to report back to the City Council within ninety (90) with a brief written update describing: (a) the updates made to succession-planning and employee-ownership materials; (b) the status of the worker-cooperative technical-assistance referral pipeline; (c) any guidance issued or planned regarding below-threshold quote-seeking from worker-owned cooperatives and related vendor-outreach steps; and (d) any additional low-cost, staff-feasible next steps the City Manager recommends for expanding support for worker-owned cooperatives in Cambridge.

vote.result: Adopted [8-0-1]

rationale.work.audience:small_business_owners: expanding support for worker-owned cooperatives in Cambridge

items.body: That the City Council go on record endorsing the May Day Strong national day of action on May 1, 2026, as an expression of Cambridge's support for workers' rights, economic justice, and the tradition of peaceful civic engagement.

vote.result: Adopted [8-0-1]

items.title: Explore commissioning a citywide housing needs study from a research institution
items.summary: A policy order asking the City Manager to explore commissioning a housing needs study through a qualified research institution to establish an evidence-based understanding of housing conditions, resident needs, and measurable goals. Charter Right exercised by Councillor Simmons.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant staff to explore options for commissioning a housing needs study through a qualified research institution, to establish a clear evidence-based understanding of existing housing conditions, resident needs, and measurable housing goals to guide future decision-making.

vote.result: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons in Council April 27, 2026

rationale.doc.status:charter_right_exercised: CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL APRIL 27, 2026
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: housing needs study to guide future decision-making
rationale.housing.tenure:any: study covers existing housing conditions overall

items.title: Halt Garden Street reconfiguration restoring two-way motor-vehicle traffic
items.summary: A policy order on the Charter Right calendar asking the City Manager to direct CDOT and other relevant departments to halt any further design, engineering, procurement, or construction activities related to reconfiguring Garden Street to restore two-way motor-vehicle traffic. Adopted on a 5-4 vote.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to direct the Cambridge Department of Transportation (CDOT) and any other relevant departments to halt any further design, engineering, procurement, or construction activities related to implementing a reconfiguration of Garden Street to restore two-way motor-vehicle traffic.

vote.result: Adopted [5-4-0]

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Flaherty in Council April 13, 2026

rationale.doc.stage:carryover: CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR FLAHERTY IN COUNCIL APRIL 13, 2026

items.title: Ordinance Committee report on Community Benefits Ordinance amendments
items.summary: The Ordinance Committee held a public hearing on March 30, 2026 to review the Community Benefits Ordinance and discuss potential updates including allowing allocation of funding to community organizations for capital projects. Voted favorably to forward the proposed amendments to the full City Council. Report Accepted and Placed on File 7-0-2 (Azeem and McGovern absent).
items.body: The Ordinance Committee held a public hearing on Monday, March 30, 2026 to review the Community Benefits Ordinance and discuss potential updates including allowing the allocation of funding to community organizations for capital projects. The Ordinance Committee voted favorably to forward the proposed amendments to the Community Benefits Ordinance to the full City Council with a favorable recommendation.

vote.result: Report Accepted and Placed on File [7-0-2]

rationale.doc.status:report_accepted: RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File [7-0-2]

items.title: Awaiting Report: open city-owned parking lots (incl. Tobin/Darby Vassal) for after-hours use
items.summary: An awaiting-report item from Councillor Nolan asking the City Manager to work with School Department, DPW, and other departments to make city-owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes including the still-under-construction Tobin/Darby Vassal complex, available for after-hours use by residents.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-29

rationale.transport.subject:parking: city owned parking lots ... made available for after-hours use
rationale.household.school:cps: focus on school complexes (Cambridge Public Schools)

items.title: Awaiting Report: outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to seek input from the community and conduct direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators, then develop additional recommendations based on the Ordinance Committee discussion.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to work with all relevant departments to seek input from the community as well as direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators and develop additional recommendations based on the discussion in the Ordinance Committee. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-51

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: short-term rental regulation under Ordinance Committee

items.title: Awaiting Report: home rule petition for Real Estate Transfer Fee
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking that the City review the previous home rule petition and prepare a new petition allowing Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee, to be sent to the state legislature.
items.body: Review the previous home rule petition and prepare a new petition that would allow Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee to be sent to the state legislature. Original Awaiting Report AR-25-69

rationale.civic.flag:home_rule_petition: home rule petition ... to be sent to the state legislature
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: Real Estate Transfer Fee policy area

items.title: Awaiting Report: restrict on-street resident parking permits in new transit-oriented developments
items.summary: An awaiting-report item on restricting eligibility for on-street resident parking permits in new transit-oriented developments.
items.body: Restricting Eligibility for On-Street Resident Parking Permits in New Transit-Oriented Developments. Original Awaiting Report 31839

rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: ties parking eligibility to residential development type

items.title: Awaiting Report: comprehensive review of safety and security at City Hall
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, including physical access controls, visitor screening, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation protocols, and surveillance systems.
items.body: That the City Manager conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, including an assessment of physical access controls, visitor screening practices, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation protocols, and the use of security technologies such as surveillance systems and alarm monitoring.

rationale.topic.area:public_safety: safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall
rationale.topic.area:civil_liberties: use of security technologies such as surveillance systems

items.title: Awaiting Report: third-party guarantors / co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking CDD to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department (CDD) to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units and provide recommendations as to whether such a policy could responsibly expand access to these units.

rationale.housing.tenure:rent: Inclusionary Housing units are rental units

items.title: Awaiting Report: review digital equity and internet access programs
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City to review its digital equity work to date — including the prior study's recommendations and steps since — to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs and report back by June 2026.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the appropriate departments to review the city's digital equity work to date, including the study's recommendations and all steps taken since the study conclusion in order to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, assessing whether residents' digital needs are or could be better provided for and to propose how we can better meet their needs and to report back to the Council by June 2026 on internet access programs.

rationale.demo.income:low: digital equity work primarily addresses low-income residents' internet access
rationale.topic.area:civil_liberties: digital equity is a civic-access issue

items.title: Awaiting Report: improve consistency of meeting information on the City's website
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to assess opportunities to improve the consistency of how meeting information is posted and accessed on the City's website.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to assess opportunities to improve the consistency of how meeting information is posted and accessed on the City's website.

rationale.civic.flag:first_timer_friendly: improve consistency of how meeting information is posted and accessed
rationale.topic.area:civil_liberties: open-government / public access to meeting info

items.title: Awaiting Report: consolidated capital lifecycle reconciliation prior to FY27 Capital Budget
items.summary: An awaiting-report item requesting that the City Manager produce and deliver a consolidated capital lifecycle reconciliation for all major capital initiatives prior to adoption of the FY27 Capital Budget. Final Actions show: Report Accepted and Placed on File.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to produce and deliver to the City Council, prior to the adoption of the FY27 Capital Budget, a consolidated capital lifecycle reconciliation for all major capital initiatives.

vote.result: Report Accepted and Placed on File

rationale.doc.status:report_accepted: RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File
rationale.fiscal.amount_bucket:above_5m: all major capital initiatives (city capital budget exceeds $5M)

items.title: Awaiting Report: simplify processes for paying election workers
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to work with the Election Commissioners to review IRS guidance and consult with other municipalities to simplify processes for paying election workers.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments as well as the Election Commissioners to review guidance from the IRS and consult with other municipalities in order to simplify our processes for paying election workers.

rationale.topic.area:civil_liberties: election worker pay processes are voting-administration
rationale.work.audience:cambridge_workers: election workers are paid Cambridge workers

items.title: Awaiting Report: expand free early child care offerings, including means-tested models
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to work with City Council and departments to expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming and exploration of state or foundation funding. Final Actions show: Report Accepted and Placed on File.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations.

vote.result: Report Accepted and Placed on File

rationale.doc.status:report_accepted: RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File
rationale.household.kids:under_5: expand free early child care offerings
rationale.demo.income:low: free early child care primarily benefits low-income families

items.title: Awaiting Report: discontinue all official City posting and engagement on X within 60 days
items.summary: An awaiting-report item directing all City departments, boards, commissions, and offices to discontinue all official posting and engagement on X within 60 days, with an explanation included for why the City will no longer use X.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to instruct all City departments, boards, commissions, and offices to discontinue all official posting and engagement on X within 60 days; and to include an explanation of why the City will no longer use X.

rationale.topic.area:civil_liberties: discontinue all official posting and engagement on X (government communications/free speech)

items.title: Awaiting Report: harmonize Harvard / MASCO institutional bus protocols with public-facing commitments
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking CDD and the Transportation Department to meet with Harvard's Office of Community Relations and the Longwood Collective (MASCO) to harmonize institutional bus protocols with public-facing commitments.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department and the Transportation Department to meet with Harvard's Office of Community Relations and the Longwood Collective (MASCO) to harmonize institutional bus protocols with public-facing commitments.

items.title: Awaiting Report: study requiring single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City to examine and report back on whether Cambridge can require single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to direct relevant City departments and staff to examine and report back on whether the city can require single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral.

rationale.demo.gender:trans: gender-neutral single-stall bathrooms primarily affect trans residents
rationale.demo.gender:non_binary: gender-neutral facilities are a primary access point for non-binary residents
rationale.topic.area:civil_liberties: bathroom access is a civil-rights issue

items.title: Awaiting Report: review legal landscape for regulating large data centers in Cambridge
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City to review the current legal landscape and provide recommendations for regulating construction of large data centers in Cambridge.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant departments to review the current legal landscape and provide recommendations for how to regulate construction of large data centers in Cambridge.

rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: regulate construction of large data centers
rationale.topic.area:climate: data centers have major energy/water footprint

items.title: Awaiting Report: raise resident parking permit fee to $75 ($25 subsidized); cap at 2; remove senior exemption
items.summary: An awaiting-report item proposing to raise the residential parking permit fee to $75 with a $25 subsidized fee for residents in affordable housing, SNAP enrollees, or low-income residents; remove the senior exemption; and cap permits at two cars per household (down from four).
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two.

rationale.demo.age:senior_65plus: remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program
rationale.demo.income:low: subsidized fee of $25 for residents who ... are low income
rationale.demo.income:means_tested: enrolled in a program such as SNAP
rationale.housing.affordability:inclusionary: residents who live in affordable housing
rationale.fiscal.kind:parking_fee: raise the fee of the parking permit program

items.title: Awaiting Report: report on artificial-turf installation decision and pause construction pending report
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to report on how the decision to install artificial turf was made, the rationale, the process for addressing community concerns and public health considerations, and to ensure construction does not move forward until the report is delivered.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested work with relevant City departments to provide a report on how the decision to install artificial turf was made, the rationale for artificial turf, and what process was taken to ensure community concerns and public health considerations were fully addressed, and to ensure that construction will not move forward until a report is delivered.

rationale.topic.area:parks: decision to install artificial turf at city fields
rationale.topic.area:public_health: public health considerations were fully addressed
